steps.title: Find $x - y$
steps.content: So $x-y=-33-(-90)=57$. That matches choice D — D is correct.

steps.title: Use the source rationale
steps.content: Choice D is correct. The first equation in the given system of equations defines $y$ as $3x+9$. Substituting $3x+9$ for $y$ in the second equation in the given system of equations yields $3(3x+9)=8x-6$. Applying the distributive property on the left-hand side of this equation yields $9x+27=8x-6$. Subtracting $8x$ from both sides of this equation yields $x+27=-6$. Subtracting $27$ from both sides of this equation yields $x=-33$. Substituting $-33$ for $x$ in the first equation of the given system of equations yields $y=3(-33)+9$, or $y=-90$. Substituting $-33$ for $x$ and $-90$ for $y$ into the expression $x-y$ yields $-33-(-90)$, or $57$.

steps.title: Check the choices
steps.content: Choice A is incorrect. This is the value of $x+y$, not $x-y$.

steps.title: Check the choices
steps.content: Choice B is incorrect. This is the value of $x$, not $x-y$.

steps.title: Check the choices
steps.content: Choice C is incorrect and may result from conceptual or calculation errors.
